definition:
1. a change, changing , (Pindar) 
2. exchange, barter, traffic , (Thucydides) 
3. (from Mid.) a transition, change , and in plural changes, vicissitudes , (Herdotus Historicus):—;with genitive change from  a thing, μ. κακῶν (Euripides); rarely change to . . , μ. ἀπραγμοσύνης (Thucydides); but this is generally expressed by a preposition, ἅμα τῇ μ. ἐς Ἕλληνας their going over  to the Greeks, (Herdotus Historicus); ἡ ἐναντία μ. change to  the contrary, (Thucydides) 
4. μ. τῆς ἡμέρης an eclipse , (Herdotus Historicus) 
5. μ. πολιτείας change  of government, a revolution , (Thucydides) 
6. as military term, a wheeling about , (Polybius Historicus); metaphorically of a speaker, (Aeschines Orator) (ML)
